unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: In this unit, your child learns the words for places in a town — the shop, the market, the school, the hospital, the mosque. They learn the people who work in these places, and simple words that tell us where things are (near, far, next to). They also meet the traffic lights and learn that red means stop and green means go. The big idea is that a town is full of helpful places and kind people, and that we all help keep our town clean and safe. The goal is warm, confident English — not perfect spelling.
unit.learningPath:
Begin with the guided teacher launch and learn a few words through pictures and play.
Listen to the Unit story and join in with repeated language.
Complete six language, speaking, early-writing and practical activities.
Meet your teacher three times each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question checkpoint and tell an adult what you can do.

readings.title: A Walk Around Town
readings.theme: town places, helpful people, safety and care
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
One sunny morning, Amal held her mother's hand. “Today we will walk around our town,” said Mum. “Come and see!”

First they came to the big road. The traffic lights were red. “Red means stop,” said Amal. They waited. Then the light was green. “Green means go!” said Amal, and they walked across.

“Look, Mum! There is my school,” said Amal. Her friend Ishmael waved from the gate. “Hello, Amal!” he called. “Hello, Ishmael!” said Amal. She smiled.

Next to the school was the market. It was very busy. A kind man was selling sweet mangoes. “Good morning,” said the shopkeeper, Adam. “Would you like a mango?”

“Yes, please,” said Amal politely. “Thank you!” Adam smiled. “You are a polite girl,” he said.

They walked past the hospital. “The doctor, Selma, works here,” said Mum. “She helps people when they are sick.” “Doctors are kind,” said Amal.

Near the park, Amal saw some litter on the ground. A little boy named Adam was dropping his paper. “Oh no,” said Amal. “Please put your paper in the bin. We keep our town clean!”

“Sorry,” said Adam. He picked up the paper and put it in the bin. “Thank you for helping,” said Amal. Now the park was clean and pretty.

Last of all, they came to the mosque. It was quiet and calm. “This is a special place,” said Mum softly. Amal's grandmother, Samira, was there. “Come, my dear,” she said, and gave Amal a warm hug.

On the way home, Mum asked, “What did you see in our town today?”

Selma counted on her fingers. “I saw the school, the market, the hospital, the park, and the mosque! I saw kind people too.”

“And what did you learn?” asked Mum.

Selma smiled a big smile. “Red means stop, green means go — and we all help to keep our town clean. I love my town!”

readings.title: Talk about City Places
readings.theme: town places, helpful people, safety and care
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript: Look, point and talk. There is a ___. The ___ is next to the ___. A ___ works at the ___. Stop. / Go. I go to the ___. Please ___. Thank you. An adult reads each line while the learner points, repeats and acts.

readings.title: City Places rhyme
readings.theme: town places, helpful people, safety and care
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
“The Traffic Lights” — say it together
Red means STOP.  /  Green means GO.  /  Yellow means WAIT,  /  Even if you're late.
Hold up a red thing, then a green thing. Freeze like a statue on “STOP” and march on the spot on “GO”. Say it three times — a little louder each time!
“I Live in a Town” — a happy song
I live in a town, yes I do,  /  I live in a town, yes I do,  /  Made by many kind hands,  /  All together built this town!
Clap along and name a place in your town after each line: “the market!”, “the school!”, “the mosque!”

grammar.title: Language pattern 1
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: There is a ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: There is a ___.

grammar.title: Language pattern 2
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: The ___ is next to the ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: The ___ is next to the ___.

grammar.title: Language pattern 3
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: A ___ works at the ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: A ___ works at the ___.

grammar.title: Language pattern 4
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: Stop. / Go.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: Stop. / Go.

grammar.title: Language pattern 5
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I go to the ___.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: I go to the ___.

grammar.title: Language pattern 6
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: Please ___. Thank you.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: Please ___. Thank you.

speaking.title: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: There is a ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern There is a ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 2 - Repeat the model
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: The ___ is next to the ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern The ___ is next to the ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: A ___ works at the ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern A ___ works at the ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - Picture talk
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Stop. / Go. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern Stop. / Go. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Role-play
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: I go to the ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I go to the ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - My confident turn
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Please ___. Thank you. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern Please ___. Thank you. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

writing.title: Writing 1 - Draw and tell
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw one idea about town places, helpful people, safety and care. Tell an adult what it shows.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 2 - Trace
writing.promptAndInstructions: Trace two useful words, saying each sound or word as you trace.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 3 - Match and copy
writing.promptAndInstructions: Match three words to pictures, then copy one word.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 4 - Label
writing.promptAndInstructions: Add two labels to your drawing or picture.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 5 - Complete a pattern
writing.promptAndInstructions: Copy and complete: I go to the ___.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 6 - Create one sentence
writing.promptAndInstructions: Choose a picture and complete one short sentence using: Please ___. Thank you.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

liveSessions.title: Meet the words
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring crayons and one real object connected to the Unit.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello song; 7 min model and repeat; 8 min game or story; 7 min guided practice; 3 min celebrate and preview
liveSessions.afterSession: Show one word, action, drawing or sentence from City Places to an adult.

liveSessions.title: Story and talk

teacherNotes.note: Use 15-20 minute independent blocks inside the lesson. Read every instruction aloud, model before expecting a response, and accept pointing, acting, drawing, tracing or speaking as valid evidence.
A teacher or caregiver remains present whenever the young learner uses recording or AI tutor features. The adult controls the device and protects the child's personal information.

shop. shop is one of the Unit words for places in a town.
market. market is one of the Unit words for places in a town.
school. school is one of the Unit words for places in a town.
hospital. hospital is one of the Unit words for places in a town.
mosque. mosque is one of the Unit words for places in a town.
There is a ___.. The approved Unit pattern is: There is a ___.
The ___ is next to the ___.. The approved Unit pattern is: The ___ is next to the ___.
A ___ works at the ___.. The approved Unit pattern is: A ___ works at the ___.
Stop. / Go.. The approved Unit pattern is: Stop. / Go.
I go to the ___.. The approved Unit pattern is: I go to the ___.

selfAssessment.statement: I can name places in a town (shop, market, school, hospital, mosque, park).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can name some people who help us (teacher, doctor, shopkeeper).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say where a place is using position words (near, next to, here, there).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say what the traffic lights mean: red means stop, green means go.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say “I go to the ______.” and “I can see a ______.”.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can join in the traffic-lights rhyme and a town song.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

rubrics.level1: Marks do not yet show the idea
rubrics.level3: Labels or copies a meaningful phrase
Uses a capital or end mark with support
rubrics.level4: Uses a capital and end mark independently
